reasoning: The informal statement asks to show that the set of open intervals (a,b) with rational a<b forms a basis that generates the standard topology on ℝ. Mathlib's `IsTopologicalBasis` (defined in `Mathlib/Topology/Bases.lean`) captures exactly this: it requires the directed-intersection property, covering the whole space, AND that `t = generateFrom s` (i.e., the ambient topology equals the one generated by the basis). Since ℝ carries its standard topology by default in Mathlib, applying `IsTopologicalBasis` to the set `{S : Set ℝ | ∃ a b : ℚ, a < b ∧ S = Ioo ↑a ↑b}` asserts both that this collection is a basis and that it generates the standard topology. The set comprehension with rational a, b coerced to ℝ correctly encodes the informal collection. Hence the formalization is logically equivalent to the informal statement.
